title: Technological Advancements and Trends in Cyber-Physical Systems for Healthcare: A Systematic Review
venue: International Conference on Advances in IoT and Security with AI
abstract: A systematic review of recent technological advancements and emerging trends in cyber-physical systems for healthcare, including IoT-enabled monitoring, security threat models, and AI-driven decision support. The paper categorises gaps in the literature and proposes a research agenda for trustworthy deployment in clinical environments.

title: Effectiveness of Game-Based Learning for Programming Courses
venue: Teaching and Learning in the Digital Era, World Scientific eBooks
abstract: This chapter evaluates the effectiveness of game-based learning interventions in undergraduate programming courses. Drawing on a multi-cohort study, it identifies design patterns that improve engagement, retention, and conceptual understanding without compromising assessment integrity.

title: Shuffled ARSH-FATI: A Novel Meta-Heuristic for Lifetime Maximization of Range-Adjustable Wireless Sensor Networks
venue: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: Introduces a shuffled variant of the ARSH-FATI meta-heuristic that jointly optimises sensor activation and transmission range to extend the operational lifetime of wireless sensor networks while preserving target coverage. Outperforms baseline approaches across multiple network sizes.

title: HyDra: Hybrid Task Mapping Application Framework for NOC-Based MPSoCs
abstract: Presents HyDra, a hybrid task-mapping framework targeting Network-on-Chip-based MPSoCs. The framework couples design-time analysis with runtime adaptation to balance performance, energy, and thermal constraints for irregular workloads.

title: ARSH-FATI: A Novel Meta-heuristic for Cluster Head Selection in Wireless Sensor Networks
abstract: Proposes ARSH-FATI, an adaptive meta-heuristic for cluster-head selection that balances residual energy, distance, and node density to extend WSN lifetime. The algorithm consistently outperforms LEACH-family baselines under heterogeneous deployments.

title: A Bandwidth-Aware Authentication Scheme for Packet-Integrity Attack Detection on Trojan Infected NoC
venue: IFIP/IEEE International Conference on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI-SoC)
abstract: A bandwidth-aware authentication design that detects packet-integrity attacks launched by hardware Trojans embedded in Network-on-Chip routers. The scheme adapts tag granularity to traffic load, holding overhead under 5% while catching all injected attacks in the evaluation set.

title: EETD: An Energy Efficient Design for Runtime Hardware Trojan Detection in Untrusted Network-on-Chip
venue: IEEE Computer Society Annual Symposium on VLSI
abstract: EETD is an energy-efficient runtime detection mechanism for hardware Trojans embedded in third-party NoC IP. By selectively authenticating high-risk traffic and reusing inflight cryptographic state, it cuts detection energy by ~40% versus prior work with no loss in detection rate.

title: A Customized Authentication Design for Traffic Hijacking Detection on Hardware-Trojan Infected NoCs
abstract: A customised lightweight authentication scheme that targets the traffic-hijacking attack class on Trojan-infected NoCs. The design uses per-flow nonces and selective integrity tags to keep area overhead minimal on FPGA prototypes.

title: Packet Leak Detection on Hardware-Trojan Infected NoCs for MPSoC Systems
venue: International Conference on Cryptography, Security and Privacy
abstract: Proposes a packet-leak detection mechanism for Trojan-infected NoCs used in MPSoC platforms. The detector watches for unauthorised packet emissions through covert side-channels and triggers per-router isolation responses.
